task_completion.description: The bcWebCam application is open and visible on the screen.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main bcWebCam application window is visible on the desktop.
A settings or Options dialog box is open over the main application, accessed via the gear icon in the bottom toolbar.

task_completion.description: The 'Copy read barcode to clipboard' option is located within the Options dialog.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'Copy read barcode to clipboard' is clearly visible next to a checkbox within the Options dialog.

task_completion.description: The 'Copy read barcode to clipboard' checkbox is checked.
task_completion.visual_evidence: There is a clear checkmark in the checkbox immediately next to the 'Copy read barcode to clipboard' text.

task_completion.description: The changes are applied by clicking OK.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OK button in the Options dialog is seen being clicked (highlighted), or the Options dialog is closed in the final screenshot after the checkbox was verified as checked.

integrity.description: The agent used the graphical user interface to change the setting.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal, command prompt, or registry editor windows are open to modify application settings directly.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the genuine bcWebCam application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The actual bcWebCam application interface is visible and interacted with, rather than a text editor modifying a configuration file.

integrity.description: The agent did not use automated scripts to bypass the UI.
integrity.visual_evidence: No Python, PowerShell, or other script files are open or running on the screen to accomplish the task.
